HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_1: **Markets** supply specific **types of resources** to villagers based on the kind of **Market Stall** built:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_2: **Food Stalls**: Supply Berries, Fish, Vegetables, Meat, Bread, and Cheese to Villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_3: **Goods Stalls**: Supply Common Clothes, Common Wares, and Candles to Villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_4: **Luxury Stalls**: Supply Herbs, Honey, Jewellery, and Gems to Villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_5: **Market Tents** can be added to **Market Stalls** to increase the **number** of **resources** for villagers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_MARKETS_TEXT_6: The **City Market** allows you to store even **more resources**, catering to the needs of **higher-density villages**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_1: Some buildings can only serve a **limited** number of **villagers** at a time, depending on the **number** and **status** of the villagers **assigned** to them:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_2: **Churches and Chapels**: Accommodate a limited number of villagers per week, depending on the Priests working there.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_3: **Hospitium**: Hosts a limited number of Pilgrims at once, determined by the number of Hostellers employed.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_4: **Lodging**: Serves a limited number of Travelers at once, depending on the Innkeepers working there.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_5: **Healing House**: Can heal a limited number of Combatants at once, depending on the Surgeons assigned.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_ATTENDANCES_TEXT_6: The **building's size** influences the **number** of **villagers** that can work inside.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_TAVERNMEAL_TEXT_1: **Tavern Meals** created in the **Tavern Kitchen** offer a temporary **Happiness** bonus when **consumed**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_TAVERNMEAL_TEXT_2: These resources don't require additional storage or distribution—any villager **visiting** a **Tavern** to fulfil their **Entertainment** needs will **automatically consume** them from the **Tavern Kitchen**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCES_SCULPTURESTAINEDGLASS_TEX: Some buildings may require **Sculptures** or **Stained Glass** as part of their construction **resources**.
These **projects** must first be produced at **dedicated workplaces** using their **listed resources** before being transported to the construction sites.
Once unlocked in the **Progression Screen**, both the **Sculpture Workshop** and the **Stained Glass Workshop** can be built at the **Builder's Workshop**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_1: **Promotion** allows you to **improve** the **efficiency** of your population at the **cost** of **additional Needs** they must fulfil.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_2: The **cost** of Promotion depends on the population's **profile**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_3: **Villagers**: Promote Serfs to Commoners or Citizens via an upfront coin cost.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_4: **Monastics**: Promote Novices to Monks or Nuns and then to Prior or Prioress using Manuscripts.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_5: **Combatants**: Promote high-ranking Militiamen to Soldiers or Knights at the cost of increased Combatant Wages.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_6: To promote **Monastics** or **Combatants** to a **higher status**, you must have a **sufficient number** at the **preceding status**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_PROMOTION_TEXT_7: You can promote your population through the **Promotion mandate** or the **villager's detailed window**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_SHORTAGE_TEXT_1: When **Essential Needs** and formerly satisfied **Additional Needs** remain unfulfilled, they eventually apply a **Happiness penalty** called **Shortage**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_SHORTAGE_TEXT_2: So long as the specific Needs remain unmet, **Shortage** penalties will **increase over time**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_SHORTAGE_TEXT_3: When a **significant portion of the population** is affected, the **Shortage** is displayed by the **overall Happiness** tooltip of the **Villager Module**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_SHORTAGE_TEXT_4: **Shortages** must be **resolved quickly**, as they often make **villagers** consider **leaving**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_SHORTAGE_TEXT_5: Once the corresponding **Need** is finally **satisfied**, its **Shortage** penalty will gradually **decay** until it **disappears**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_1: As villagers are **promoted** to **higher statuses**, their **housing requirements** also increase in **quality**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_2: **Serfs**: Low-Quality Housing has no specific requirements.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_3: **Commoners**: Medium-Quality Housing must be within Beautification coverage and away from Undesirable Neighbourhoods.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_4: **Citizens**: High-Quality Housing must be near Paved Roads while meeting the Medium-Quality Housing requirements.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_5: For **houses** to **upgrade** in quality, there must be villagers of the **appropriate status** requiring them.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGQUALITY_TEXT_6: **Higher-quality** housing also **requires** more **advanced resources** to construct.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDENSITY_TEXT_1: Houses with **higher Density** allow **more people** to live within the **same housing area**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDENSITY_TEXT_2: **Increased population** in one location reduces **travel time**, speeds up **tax collection**, and lets you save on **Territory Upkeep** costs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDENSITY_TEXT_5: **Medium-Density**: Must be within Light Fortification and Patrol coverage.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDENSITY_TEXT_6: **High-Density**: Must be within Heavy Fortification and Patrol coverage.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDENSITY_TEXT_7: **Higher-density** housing requires **more** of the same **resources** to construct.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_BEAUTIFICATION_TEXT_1: **Housing** must be within a **Beautification area** to upgrade to **Medium-Quality** or **higher**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_BEAUTIFICATION_TEXT_2: **Beautification** areas are created by constructing **decorative parts** that aren't **tied to any estate**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_BEAUTIFICATION_TEXT_3: **Combining** different **types** of decorations—such as trees, benches, bushes, and fountains—**expands** the **coverage** of a Beautification area.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_BEAUTIFICATION_TEXT_4: You can find these decorative parts in the **Build Menu** under **Common Decoration**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_UNDESIRABLENEIGHBORHOOD_TEX: **Undesirable Neighbourhood** areas are created by **certain workplaces** that your population considers too **dirty** or **noisy**, such as the **Iron Smelter** and **Sawmill**.
These **workplaces** generate a **small Undesirable Neighbourhood area** that **expands** as **more** of these workplaces are **grouped together**.
While **Serfs** don't mind, it's best to **avoid** placing these workplaces near the **higher-quality housing** of **Commoners** and **Citizens**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_PAVEDROAD_TEXT_1: **Higher-Quality Housing** requires **Paved Roads**, which are created by providing **Stone** resources to the **Paviours** and having them **upgrade dirt paths** using the **Road Paving** painting.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_PAVEDROAD_TEXT_2: Over time, **Paved Roads** **decay** and must be **repaired** by **Paviours** using additional **Stone**. While **Paviours** are focused on **repairs**, they **cannot upgrade** new dirt paths.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_PAVEDROAD_TEXT_3: To **efficiently** manage **large areas**, consider building **multiple Paviours' Yards**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDOWNGRADE_TEXT_1: For a **House** to maintain its **quality** and **density**, its current housing **requirements** must remain **fulfilled**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDOWNGRADE_TEXT_2: Should any of these **requirements** become **unfulfilled**, a warning **Downgrade Icon** will indicate that the House will eventually lose its quality or density.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDOWNGRADE_TEXT_3: Selecting the House will show which **requirement** is failing and how much **time** remains before the **downgrade** occurs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDOWNGRADE_TEXT_4: If a House **downgrades**, villagers may still **occupy it**, provided the House's **Quality** still **meets their Housing Need**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_HOUSING_HOUSINGDOWNGRADE_TEXT_5: A **downgraded** House can be **upgraded again** once its **requirements** are **fulfilled**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_SLOTS_TEXT_1: **Edicts** and **Privileges** are policies that grant context-specific **bonuses** to your village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_SLOTS_TEXT_2: To enact them, you must first unlock **Edict** and **Privilege slots** through the **Progression Screen**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_SLOTS_TEXT_3: **Edict Slots**: Unlocked via the Common Path.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_SLOTS_TEXT_4: **Privilege Slots**: Unlocked through each of the Estate Paths.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_SLOTS_TEXT_5: Once a slot is unlocked, you can **select** one of the desired **Edicts** or **Privileges** from the **Edicts & Privileges** page in the **Royal Book**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_1: Each **Edict** and **Privilege** offers unique effects that can **benefit** your village in **specific situations**. Here are a few examples:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_2: **Careful Planning**: An Edict that refunds some of the Tools used during construction.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_3: **Dutiful Payers**: A Labour Privilege that reduces the Happiness penalty from taxation.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_4: **Inherited Land**: A Kingdom Privilege that occasionally grants you a free Territory.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_5: **Tithe Taxes**: A Clergy Privilege that allows you to tax Chapels and Churches.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_EFFECTS_TEXT_6: As your village prospers, you may also unlock **secret Privileges**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_CHANGING_TEXT_1: Once an **Edict** or **Privilege** is **enacted**, it becomes **permanently locked**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_CHANGING_TEXT_2: However, you can unlock a previously locked **Edict** or **Privilege** by utilising the **Revise an Edict** or **Revise an Estate Privilege** mandates.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_CHANGING_TEXT_3: Your **Bailiff**, **Monastic Emissary**, or **Royal Courtier** can perform this **mandate**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_CHANGING_TEXT_4: Revising an **Edict** or **Privilege** allows you to **remove** it, **freeing** up the **slot** for a new selection.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EDICTSPRIVILEGES_CHANGING_TEXT_5: Remember that only **one Edict** or **Privilege** can be **revised** per **mandate**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_1: In addition to the **Residential Taxes**, you can generate **additional** coin **revenue** through other **types** of **taxation**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_2: **Milling Taxes**: Tax the production of flour.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_3: **Mining Taxes**: Tax the mining of minerals.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_4: **Smithing Taxes**: Tax the production of tools and weapons.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_5: **Drinking Taxes**: Tax the consumption of drinks at the Tavern.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_6: **Tithe Taxes**: Tax services at Churches and Chapels.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_7: These additional taxes can be **unlocked** through the **Progression Screen** or obtained via **Privileges**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAVERN_TEXT_1: Villagers can fulfil their **Entertainment** Needs by visiting the **Tavern**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAVERN_TEXT_2: They will only do so if **bógyóbor**, **Beer**, or **Wine** are **stored** there. This requires assigning a **Taverner** to the **Service Counter**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_TAVERN_TEXT_3: In addition to stocking the necessary resources, ensure your **Public Lounge** has **enough space** to **accommodate** all **visitors**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_LODGING_TEXT_1: The **Tavern** can be expanded with an **Inn Lodging**, allowing you to **host** weary **travellers** from across the realm.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_LODGING_TEXT_2: Once your **Inn Lodging** is **functional**, your **first traveller** will arrive. If you **allow** them to **spread the word** about your village, **more travellers** will pay a **visit**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_LODGING_TEXT_3: You can **earn** a fair sum of **coins** for each traveller by meeting **all** their **needs**. However, **poorly attended travellers** may **leave without paying**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_COMPANIES_TEXT_1: To **recruit** Combatants, you must form **Companies** at the **Castle Keep** or via **Encampments**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_COMPANIES_TEXT_2: Each Company **specializes** in a specific type of **weapon**, with the **Castle Company** focusing on **Spears**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_COMPANIES_TEXT_3: It's **important** to form a **variety** of **Companies**, as later **Military Missions** will require **multiple Company types**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_COMPANIES_TEXT_4: **Companies** have a **base capacity** of **Combatants**, which can be **increased** by constructing a **Command Post**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_QUARTERS_TEXT_1: **Combatants** can be **housed** in the **Castle** using the following buildings:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_QUARTERS_TEXT_2: **Barracks**: Provides compact accommodations for Militiamen and Soldiers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_QUARTERS_TEXT_3: **Private Quarters**: Offers spacious accommodations for Knights.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_QUARTERS_TEXT_4: The **capacity** of these buildings is determined by their **size**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_QUARTERS_TEXT_5: If Combatants **lack access** to a **Healing House**, they will recover from wounds at their **quarters**, though at a much **slower rate**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_FITTING_TEXT_1: A Company's **power** is measured by **Might**, which is determined by **several factors**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_FITTING_TEXT_6: The **Strength** of each **Combatant** is influenced by:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_FITTING_TEXT_9: Training State or whether they are Wounded
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_FITTING_TEXT_10: A **well-prepared** Company stands a much better chance of **surviving** dangerous **missions**, so be sure to make the most of them!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_1: **Companies** can undertake **Military Missions**, which offer a variety of **rewards**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_2: When deployed on a **Military Mission**, a Company **travels** away for a **few days**. Upon their **return**, Combatants bring back a portion of the promised **rewards** and then **heal** and **recuperate** at the Castle.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_3: **After** a few days, the Company **resumes** their **Military Mission** outside the village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_4: After completing **four** such **cycles**, the **Company** fully **returns** to the village and is **ready** for its next **assignment**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_5: The **better** a Company **recuperates**, the **better** it **performs**, resulting in a **greater final reward**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_MISSION_TEXT_6: Progressively more **difficult Military Missions** can be **unlocked** by successfully **completing previous** ones.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_ROYALHOSTING_TEXT_1: After founding your **Castle**, forming your **first Company**, and achieving sufficient **Prosperity**, you will be invited to **host** and **attend** to **Royal Companies** at your Castle.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_ROYALHOSTING_TEXT_2: These weary guests will require the **same needs** as your **Combatants**, along with time to **heal** and **train**. Ensure you have enough **Training Dummies** to accommodate them!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_ROYALHOSTING_TEXT_3: By dealing with **Royal Companies**, you'll earn **Coins** and gain **Kingdom Influence**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_DUTY_TEXT_3: **Patrol Duty**: Provide Patrol Coverage over designated Patrolling areas.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_DUTY_TEXT_4: **Guard Duty**: Protect Active Fortification buildings, such as Watchtowers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_DUTY_TEXT_5: A Company's **Duty** can be **changed** at **any time**, provided the Company is available.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_1: As you **complete** increasingly challenging **Military Missions**, you will **unlock Higher-Grade** Military Missions.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_2: These **high-difficulty missions** can only be undertaken by **Military Companies** of **equal or higher Grades**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_3: **1st Grade Company**: Composed entirely of Militiamen.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_4: **2nd Grade Company**: Must include at least one Soldier.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_5: **3rd Grade Company**: Must include at least one Knight.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_KINGDOM_GRADE_TEXT_6: A Company will **lose** its **Grade** if it **no longer** has the required **high-status Combatant**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_DEVOTING_TEXT_1: **Monastics** are villagers who dedicate their lives to a **monastery**, becoming **Monks** or **Nuns**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_DEVOTING_TEXT_2: To **devote** villagers, construct **Dorms** and select the **"Devote a villager to the monastery"** option in the **Building Window**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_DEVOTING_TEXT_4: **Men** can only be assigned to **Monk Dorms**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_DEVOTING_TEXT_6: Ensure you have **enough villagers** of the appropriate **gender** before **constructing** the corresponding **Dorms**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_WORKPLACE_TEXT_3: **Monastic Kitchen**: Prepares monastic meals for Monks and Nuns.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_WORKPLACE_TEXT_6: **Winery**: Uses Barrels and Grapes to produce Wine.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_WORKPLACE_TEXT_7: Monastics are **automatically assigned** to workplaces within their monastery, with **higher-status** Monastics **prioritizing** roles such as **Monastic Emissary** and **Refectorians** over **Farmers** and **Copyists**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_MANUSCRIPT_TEXT_1: **Copyists** working in the **Scriptorium** produce **Manuscripts** which serve several purposes:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_MANUSCRIPT_TEXT_2: **Promoting** Novices to Brothers or Sisters and then to Prior or Prioress.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_MANUSCRIPT_TEXT_4: **Delivering** to the Bishop in exchange for valuable resources.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_MANUSCRIPT_TEXT_5: **Manuscripts** take **time** to produce, so ensure your **Scriptorium** is **large enough** to meet your needs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_NEEDS_TEXT_2: **Cloister**: Build a Cloister near Dorms and workplaces to provide Monastics a place to visit and reflect.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_NEEDS_TEXT_3: **Monastic Food**: Prepare various Monastic meals at the Monastic Kitchen using resources from both the village and the monastery.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_NEEDS_TEXT_6: **Brothers and Sisters**: Access to the Cloister and two Monastic Food.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_NEEDS_TEXT_7: **Prior and Prioress**: Access to the Cloister and three Monastic Food.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_PILGRIMS_TEXT_1: **Pilgrims** are travellers on a **spiritual journey** to distant landmarks of the Faith.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_PILGRIMS_TEXT_2: They will begin **visiting** your monastery once you have a **functional Hospitium** and have welcomed your first **wandering monk**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_PILGRIMS_TEXT_3: You can **assist Pilgrims** by offering them shelter at your **Hospitium** and **resupplying** them for their journey.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_PILGRIMS_TEXT_4: In return, Pilgrims may leave **donations** of **valuable resources**, such as **Manuscripts**, **Coins**, **Candles**, and various types of **Food**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_IMPROVING_TEXT_1: You can **increase** the **productivity** of various **workplaces**, such as the **Lumber Camp** and the **Wheat Farm**, by making the following **improvements**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_IMPROVING_TEXT_2: **Add Worker Capacity**: Allows more villagers to work at the same workplace.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_IMPROVING_TEXT_3: **Add Output Storage**: Expands the capacity for storing produced resources at the workplace.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_IMPROVING_TEXT_4: **Improving workplaces** is a cost-effective way to **save** **resources** and **space**, as Improvement Parts are generally **smaller** and **less expensive** than the original Building Parts.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_IMPROVING_TEXT_5: **Add Input Storage**: Expands the capacity for storing resources required by the workplace.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWTERRITORY_TEXT_1: You must **purchase** additional **Territories** to **reach** distant **resources** and **expand** your **village**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWTERRITORY_TEXT_2: This can be achieved by **clicking** the **Territory** button in the **Action Module** at the bottom centre of the screen.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWTERRITORY_TEXT_3: **Purchasing Territories** requires an **upfront cost** and incurs a recurring **upkeep**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWTERRITORY_TEXT_4: Only buy what you need, as the **upkeep** gradually **increases** with **each** additional **Territory**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_TRAVELDISTANCE_TEXT_1: As your **village grows**, longer **travel distances** can **slow down** your villagers and **reduce productivity**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_TRAVELDISTANCE_TEXT_2: To **minimise delays**, ensure that villagers producing critical resources **live close** to their **workplaces** and have **easy access** to all their **Needs nearby**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_TRAVELDISTANCE_TEXT_4: Establishing **new** village **Hubs** and managing **resource flow** are **effective strategies** to minimise delays.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWHUBS_TEXT_1: You must establish new **village hubs** to exploit **distant resources** like **Mineral Deposits**, **Fish**, and **Meat**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWHUBS_TEXT_2: This requires building **essential structures**, including **workplaces**, **houses**, a **Market**, a **Well**, a **Church**, and a **Granary**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWHUBS_TEXT_3: Villagers prioritize **nearby buildings** to fulfil their **Needs**, ensuring they stay within their new hubs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_NEWHUBS_TEXT_4: To maintain a consistent resource flow between villages, use **Storage Priorities** to direct your Transporters efficiently.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_HOSTING_TEXT_2: **Labour**: Host travellers at your Inn Lodge.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_HOSTING_TEXT_3: **Kingdom**: Host royal companies in your Barracks and Private Quarters.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_HOSTING_TEXT_5: You are **rewarded** for ensuring that **guests leave** your village with **high approval**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_OPPORTUNITIES_HOSTING_TEXT_6: **Approval** is **gained** through **high Happiness**, which is achieved by **fulfilling** all of the **guests' needs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_1: At the start of a **new game**, you **choose** the **aspiration** that shapes your journey:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_2: **Play On Your Own**: Freeform gameplay with no aspiration story.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_3: **Build a Prestigious Burg**: Earn a City Charter and achieve sovereignty.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_4: **Build a Prosperous Priory**: Construct a Priory and establish flourishing trade across the realm.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_5: **Build a Mighty Stronghold**: Construct an impressive castle and fight for the King.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_NEWGAME_TEXT_6: **Aspirations** can **only** be **selected** before beginning a **new game**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_QUESTS_TEXT_1: As you progress through **story aspirations**, you'll receive **aspiration objectives** and **milestone quests** to complete.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_QUESTS_TEXT_2: Each **aspiration objective** includes **progression** and **failure conditions** that will **influence** your village's **development** over time.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_QUESTS_TEXT_3: These **long-term objectives** are gradually achieved as you complete the **short-term milestone** quests.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_COMPLETION_TEXT_1: Once you have **completed all** the **objectives** of an **aspiration**, you can continue **playing freely** with your village.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_ASPIRATION_COMPLETION_TEXT_2: At this stage, you are **free** to **deviate** from the previous **aspiration objectives** and shape the **village** of your **dreams**!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_1: **Before** starting your new **village**, you can choose a **Game Rule Preset** to **tailor** your **experience**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_3: **Creative**: Disables challenging game elements to focus on building and creativity.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_4: **Casual**: Offers a relaxed experience, ideal for learning with minimal challenge.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_5: **Classic**: Provides balanced challenges, reflecting the intended game experience.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_6: **Challenge**: Features heightened difficulty for advanced players.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_7: **Game Rule Presets cannot** be changed **after** your village is **created**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_8: **Achievements** are only **available** in **Classic**, **Challenge**, or similar **Custom** presets.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_PRESETS_TEXT_9: **Some Achievements** require the game to be set to **Challenge** or a **Custom** preset with similarly **high difficulty** settings.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_1: When selecting your **Game Rule Preset**, you can also **modify individual rules** to customize your experience:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_3: Build instantly, at no cost, and control the visual progression of construction steps.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_4: Own all Territories at no cost.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_5: Adjust the regrowth rate of Environmental Resources.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_6: Modify the production time of workplaces.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_7: Alter the risk level of hazards in Military Missions.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_8: **Changing** an individual **Game Rule** will automatically set your **Preset** to **Custom**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GAMERULES_RULES_TEXT_9: Keep in mind that **Achievements** will be **disabled** if the **Game Rules** are set **easier** than the **requirements** of the chosen **Game Rule Preset**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTWATCHPOST_TEXT_: **Watchposts** let you assign Villagers to the job of **Patroller** and **Watchmen**, to provide **Safety** and **Fortification** to your village.
**Patrollers** patrol in **zones** marked for Patrolling, to provide **Safety** to surrounding **Houses**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTTAVERN_TEXT_1: Taverns provide **Entertainment** to villagers who have the **Entertainment Need**. They can also lodge **Visitors**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTTAVERN_TEXT_2: A **Service Counter** and a **Public Lounge** are required to welcome **Villagers**, but you will need to provide them with drinks, such as **bógyóbor**, **Beer** or **Wine**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTMONASTERY_TEXT_: **Monasteries** are monuments that work differently from others, as they **automatically** dispatch their **Monastics** between their different **workplaces**.
Build a **Dormitory** to house Monks or Nuns.
**Devote** **Monks** and/or **Nuns** to the **Monastery**. Be careful: Monastics are **devoted for life**.
Build a **Herb Garden** to produce the valuable **Herb** resource.
Build a **Scriptorium** to produce the beneficial **Manuscript** resource.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCOMPANY_TEXT_2: To **create** and prepare your first Company:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCOMPANY_TEXT_3: Click the **“Add a Company”** button on your **Castle Keep** and assign villagers. Be careful: **Combatants** are **dedicated for life**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCOMPANY_TEXT_4: Store **weapons** to an **Armory** so your Combatants can equip themselves for combat.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCOMPANY_TEXT_5: It is recommended to wait for your Combatants to be **fully trained** before sending them to **Missions**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCOMPANY_TEXT_6: **Assign** and **Deploy** your Company to a **Mission** using the **Army Book**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTMANDATES_TEXT_1: The **Great Hall** enables you to enact **Mandates**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTMANDATES_TEXT_2: At any point, you can start the **Levy Mandate** to gain coins at the expense of villagers' Happiness or use the **Promote Mandate** to raise villagers to higher statuses.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTMANDATES_TEXT_4: **Access** the **Mandates** from the **Royal Book** or directly from your **Great Hall**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTFORTIFICATION_T: **Fortification** is required by **Houses** to increase their **Density**, and by certain **Workplaces** to **function** properly.
There are two ways to provide fortification to surroundings:
By building **Watchtowers**. You then need a **Watchman** to occupy it. They can be hired from a **Watchpost**, a **sub-building** of the **Manor House**.
By **enclosing** an area with **Walls** that provides the type of **Fortification** needed.
You should see a **visual feedback** once a area is covered with Fortification.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTDECORATION_TEXT: **For Beautification**: Build them from the Build menu.
**For Splendour**: Build them from any Monument of the Estates.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_INCREASINGPROSPERITY: You can **increase Prosperity** in numerous ways, such as gaining **more profit**, having **more population**, and **unlocking** and **upgrading Trade Routes**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_HOSTINGGUESTS_TEXT_1: Each **Estate** allows you to **host guests**, offering various **rewards**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_HOSTINGGUESTS_TEXT_4: Let **Travellers** spread the word of your **Inn Lodge**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_HOSTINGGUESTS_TEXT_5: Be summoned by the **King** after building **Barracks** at your **Castle**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTBRIDGE_TEXT_3: Make sure that the **whole Bridge** is located **within** your **territories**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTBRIDGE_TEXT_4: You can decide its **length** and **rotation** by **moving** its **end parts**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTBRIDGE_TEXT_5: You can **move** and **scale** the Bridge by **selecting the entire building** via double-click.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCASTLE_TEXT_1: **Castles** serve as fortified monuments and house **Military Companies**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCASTLE_TEXT_2: To establish a Castle, start by building a **Castle Keep**. This will allow you to create your first **Company**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_GETTINGSTARTED_FIRSTCASTLE_TEXT_6: An **Armory**, so your combatants can equip **Weapons**
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_BUILDINGPLACEMENT_TEX: While **placing buildings**, pay attention to their surrounding **blue borders**.
These **borders** define the buildings' **collision zones**, which means the following:
Blue borders are usually accompanied by **white arrows**: these indicate building **entrances**.
Villagers will **not walk** past **blue borders** **unless** they have a **reason** to **access** the buildings.
**Two** different **borders** crossing each other will **block** villagers' **paths**.
A **red** **arrow** means the entrance is **blocked** and may **prevent** villagers from **accessing** the building, so always ensure a building's white **arrow** entrances remain **white**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_BUILDINGFORTIFICATION: **Fortification** is a **coverage effect** given by certain buildings, enabled either as **Active** or **Passive**:
**Active Fortification** requires a Watchman or a Combatant on Guard Duty to be enabled.
**Passive Fortification** is enabled as soon as the structure is built.
**Walls** and **Monument Towers** (such as Manor Houses, Rustic Churches, or Castles) provide **Passive Fortification**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_MONUMENTBASIC_ACTIVEFORTIFICATION_T: For **Watchtowers** or **Guard Locations** to provide **Active Fortification**, you must assign Villagers to the **Watchman** job at the **Watchpost** or set a **Military Company** to **Guard Duty**.
Once **assigned**, these villagers will **automatically** visit **Watchtowers** and **Guard Locations**. This will cause the buildings to grant **Active Fortification**.
If **Watchtowers** or **Guard Locations** are left **unvisited** for **several days**, they will **stop** granting **Fortifications**.
They will only **resume** granting **Fortification** once **visited again**.
To **keep** these buildings **functional**, make sure that:
Their **Watchposts** or **Encampments** are located **near** your **Watchtowers** or **Guard Locations**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_1: **Villagers**, **Monastics**, and **Combatants** spend their time between **working** and **resting**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_2: While **working**, **Villagers** and **Monastics** head to their **workplace**, while **Combatants** will attend to their assigned **Duty**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_3: After working, they take time to rest and fill their Needs:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_4: They seek to **fill** unfulfilled or soon-to-be **unfulfilled** Needs.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_5: **Needs** that cannot be filled are left **unanswered** and may trigger **Happiness penalties**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERSCHEDULE_TEXT_6: If Villagers have resting **time left** after addressing their Needs, they may **return** to their **Homes**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERMOVEMENT_TEXT_1: **Villagers travel** around the village to **fill** their **Needs** and **perform** their **Jobs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERMOVEMENT_TEXT_2: When filling their **Needs**, Villagers will seek resources **closer** to their **House**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERMOVEMENT_TEXT_3: When looking for a **Job**, Villagers will seek workplaces with matching Jobs **close** to their **House**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERMOVEMENT_TEXT_4: In addition, if a Villager's **Job** becomes **too distant** from their **House**, they will **relocate** to a better position. **Villagers** will **not move** into a **House** located **too far** from their **workplace**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_VILLAGERS_VILLAGERMOVEMENT_TEXT_5: Additionally, **Villagers** prefer **travelling** on **existing paths** rather than creating new ones.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_LABORER_ADDITIONALTAXATION_TEXT_8: **Additional Taxation** comes at the cost of **reducing** your **Labour Influence Cap**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_CLOISTER_TEXT_1: The **Cloister** is an **enclosed courtyard** partially **surrounded** by a **walkway**. It is an **essential** part of the **Monastery**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_CLOISTER_TEXT_2: To properly **build** one, you must **meet** the following **requirements**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_CLOISTER_TEXT_3: Place parts of **Cloister Hall** and **Cloister Garden Entrance**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_CLOISTER_TEXT_4: Form a large **enclosed area** completely **surrounded** by **Monastery Parts** and has with **Interior Access**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_CLERGY_CLOISTER_TEXT_5: Ensure the **enclosed area** is **accessible** from the **Interior Access**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_RESOURCEFLOW_RESOURCEAVAILABILITIES: As **resources** flow through your villages, they **shift** between different **states**.
These count towards your **total available resources**, as displayed on the **Resource Module** at the top of your screen.
**Storage**: Resources located in storage are available for Quests and workplaces.
**Production Output**: Villagers can fetch resources from workplaces' Output Storage.
**Stalls**: Once in Stalls, resources can only be consumed and cannot be removed for Quests or production.
**In Transit**: Resources transferred to stalls or storages cannot be used while in transit.
These are **reserved** by villagers and are **not counted** in the total number of resources.
**Production Input**: Resources moved into workplaces' Input Storage cannot be used anymore.
**Construction**: Resources spent on construction cannot be used anymore.
**In Transit**: Resources transferred to workplaces or constructions cannot be used while transported.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_1: **Start small** and only build what you require. It is **easy** to get carried away early on and **overspend** beyond what **small villages** can afford.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_3: **Keep** your **expenses minimal** until you have a stable income.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_4: Once you **start earning** sufficient **Coins**, you should:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_5: **Unlock production chains** to produce and sell new resources.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_6: **Build** your first **Monuments** and earn Splendour to reach new Unlockables.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_EARLYBUDGET_TEXT_7: **Promote** your **population** and address their new needs to increase your taxation power.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TOOLS_TEXT_1: **Tools** are one of the most **essential resources** for your village. They are **required** for most **constructions** and play a **critical** role in certain **production chains**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TOOLS_TEXT_1_1: For this reason, it's best to **purchase Tools** early on.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TOOLS_TEXT_2: Better yet, producing Tools is a **valuable investment**, as it **reduces** your **reliance** on expensive **traders**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TOOLS_TEXT_3: And if you produce more than you need, you can **sell** your **surplus** back for a hefty income!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_1: **Forests** offer plenty of **renewable resources** to those who manage them wisely:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_2: Supplement your **Lumber Camps** with **Forester Camp** to control deforestation.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_3: Avoid cutting **dense forests**, as they are ideal hunting grounds for Boars.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_4: **Berries** grow on forest borders and by light woodland patches.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_5: **Livestock** can graze in forested pastures as well, outside of painted **Reforestation areas**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_FORESTMANAGEMENT_TEXT_6: Balancing these **workplaces** will help you **save** valuable **space**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_WORKPLACEOPTIMIZATION_TEXT: There are many ways to **increase** the **production speed** of a workplace:
**Promoting Villagers** if the workplace is suited for a promoted status.
When **increasing** the **productivity** of a workplace, be mindful of its has **impact** on **input resources** and other related **workplaces**.

**For example**, assigning more **Bakers** will increase the need for **Flour**. Producing more **Flour** requires more **Wheat**, which is accomplished by growing **larger fields**. These require more **Territory** space.
**Start** by **assigning** only a **few Villagers** at a time and **progressively increase** their numbers to **match** your **needs**.
In addition, if you have a **surplus** of **Coins**, you can **buy resources** from **Traders** to resolve **resource shortages** in your **production chains**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_1: As your **village prospers**, your **population** will **grow**, and so its **needs**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_2: To **manage** a growing **population**, keep an eye on the following:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_3: **Residential Areas**: Increase density to avoid unnecessarily large zones.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_4: **Resource Consumption**: Maintain a steady surplus to prevent shortages.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_5: **Market Distribution**: Build multiple markets for the same resource, especially in large or dense villages.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_6: **Church Space**: Plan ahead, as churches will always be in demand.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_7: **Travel Time**: Observe your villagers to see if anyone is travelling too far.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_POPULATIONGROWTH_TEXT_8: It's never too late to **relocate** buildings and **adjust** areas if something goes wrong!
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TREASURYDEFICIT_TEXT_1: A **treasury deficit** occurs when your **expenses** exceed your **income** long enough to **drain** all available **Coins**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TREASURYDEFICIT_TEXT_2: There are several ways to **resolve** this, such as:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_TREASURYDEFICIT_TEXT_9: Even with a **negative treasury**, **expenses** continue to be **paid**, widening the gap further. You must **act quickly** before the deficit grows too large to **overcome**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_FORTIFICATION_FORTIFICATIONTYPES_TE: Buildings providing **Fortification** either give **Light** or **Heavy** Fortification:
**Light Fortification** is granted by structures such as wooden Fortifications, Church Bell Towers, and Manor House Towers.
**Heavy Fortification** is granted by more imposing buildings, such as stone-built Fortifications and Castles.
Buildings that require **Light Fortification** will **benefit from Heavy Fortification**, but not vice versa.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_IMPROVINGPATROLS_TEXT_1: There are many ways to **improve** your **patrols**:
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_IMPROVINGPATROLS_TEXT_2: **Add Patrollers** to increase their **Patrol Efficiency**, which improves their range and their effect on the **Patrol Coverage** of housing.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_IMPROVINGPATROLS_TEXT_3: **Adjust the size** of each **Patrol Area** to fit the **Patrol Efficiency** of your available Patrollers.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_IMPROVINGPATROLS_TEXT_5: **Patrol Efficiency** has a **sweet spot**: past a certain number of patrollers, **adding** more workers will **barely improve** their **range** and **effect**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_STRATEGY_IMPROVINGPATROLS_TEXT_6: Therefore, **instead** of **stretching Patrol Efficiency** and **Patrol Area** to cover distant housing, consider **building** new **Watchposts** and **Encampments**.
HELP_TOPICS_CONTENT.HELP_EXPANDING_TRAVELDISTANCE_TEXT_3: If a **Need** is situated **too far** from their workplace, villagers may be **unable** to **fulfil** it, **negatively** affecting their **Happiness**.
